MILESTONE: TIES SPS ENGINE MAXIMUM (E=8) -- FIRST E=8 ENTRY IN SPS SERIES
mechanism: Pacific winter swell breaking on outer coast: wave face collapse + white water roar + infrasound + spray
season: Winter (November-March primary; October and April significant)

e8_scale.definition: SPS maximum: most acoustically energetic routine natural event in the Pacific Northwest
e8_scale.not_extraordinary: Not volcanic eruption or major earthquake. Predictable annual winter event.
e8_scale.why_outer_coast: Puget Sound protected by Olympic Peninsula and Strait of Juan de Fuca. Outer coast = unprotected Pacific exposure.

storm_generation.formation_location: North Pacific storm track: Japan → Aleutian Islands → Pacific Northwest coast
storm_generation.peak_season: November-March (temperature differential greatest; strongest low-pressure systems)
storm_generation.fetch_miles: Thousands (North Pacific is largest ocean basin; longest available fetch to any coastline)
storm_generation.swell_organization: Wind waves → organized long-period swell over travel distance

wave_data.hs_definition: Significant wave height: average of highest one-third of observed waves
wave_data.energy_scaling: Wave energy ~ Hs^2 * period. 25-ft / 15-sec wave >> 5-ft / 5-sec wave by large multiple.

cape_disappointment.location: Mouth of Columbia River, southern outer coast
cape_disappointment.lighthouse_note: Oldest working lighthouse on Pacific coast
cape_disappointment.columbia_river_bar: One of most dangerous in world; 'Graveyard of the Pacific'; closed to shipping in major wave events
cape_disappointment.coast_guard: Cape Disappointment Coast Guard Station trains national rescue swimmers

long_beach_peninsula.note: One of longest beaches in US. Pacific-facing, unobstructed. Storm watching tourism.

kalaloch_ruby_beach.features: Sea stacks, wave refraction, complex acoustic amplification
kalaloch_ruby_beach.ruby_beach: One of most dramatic coastal landscapes in continental US

olympic_coast_roadless.note: One of longest undeveloped coastlines in lower 48

cape_flattery.location: Northwestern tip of Olympic Peninsula and contiguous US
cape_flattery.function: Entrance to Strait of Juan de Fuca

acoustic_physics.initial_crash: Wave face collapse: crest pitches forward, trapped air compresses suddenly. Low-frequency concussive. Felt in chest before heard as sound.
acoustic_physics.roar: White water cascade up beach: aerated turbulent broadband noise. Sustained ambient between crashes.
acoustic_physics.infrasound: Wave-wave interaction + pressure oscillations generate sub-20 Hz. Propagates through ocean and ground. Detectable on global seismometer networks as microseismic noise.
acoustic_physics.spray: Saltwater spray at high wind: high-frequency hiss overlaid on low-frequency roar.
acoustic_physics.acoustic_footprint_km: 2-3 radius at E=8. Conversation impossible at shoreline.
acoustic_physics.hearing_protection: Appropriate for extended exposure at shoreline during major storm surf

storm_arc.arc_logic: Pacific storm energy enters at maximum on outer coast (256) and expresses through each ecosystem inward
storm_arc.relationship_to_abiotic_arc: Abiotic arc 245-252: E=3-5 moderate natural sounds. Storm arc 256-260: E=6-8 maximum meteorological expressions. Same categories at maximum energy.

cultural_context.makah_nation: Only tribal group in lower 48 with treaty rights protecting ocean whale hunting. Traditional ocean canoe whaling. Deepest human engagement with Pacific winter swells in PNW.
cultural_context.columbia_bar_pilots: Small specialized guild boarding ships at Columbia River bar. Bar closed to shipping in major wave events. E=8 surf is their primary occupational hazard.
cultural_context.storm_watching_tourism: Coastal lodges promote 'storm season' as tourist attraction. Kalaloch Lodge, Long Beach Peninsula, coastal Washington and Oregon.
cultural_context.graveyard_of_pacific: Historical maritime disaster zone: Cape Disappointment and adjacent Oregon coast

theory_nodes.concept: TIES SPS ENGINE MAXIMUM (E=8). First E=8 entry in SPS series. Definition: most acoustically energetic routine annual event in PNW natural soundscape. Pacific winter storm surf on outer coast meets this definition: not volcanic or seismic extraordinary event, but predictable seasonal occurrence. Cape Disappointment, Kalaloch, Ruby Beach, Olympic coast.
theory_nodes.description: E=8 SPS maximum: routine winter storm surf, outer coast.

theory_nodes.concept: Infrasound from heavy surf: sub-20 Hz generated by wave-wave interaction and pressure oscillations. Propagates through ocean and ground as microseismic noise. Detectable on global seismometer networks. The winter storm surf at Cape Disappointment generates seismic signal measurable worldwide.
theory_nodes.description: Global seismic signature of Pacific storm surf.

theory_nodes.concept: STORM ARC 256-260 opens. Pacific storm energy documented from outer coast (256, ocean) inward: storm wind (257), mountain snowstorm (258), lightning (259), closing entry (260). The arc is the acoustic record of a Pacific storm passing through the Pacific Northwest landscape from coast to interior.
theory_nodes.description: Storm Arc 256-260: Pacific storm energy from coast to interior.

cross_references.relationship: Anthropogenic arc closes (255); Storm Arc opens (256). From working forest to maximum ocean. E=3 to E=8. The most dramatic energy step in the SPS series.

Both coastal water sound. 252: moderate energy (E=4), tidal surge on shore, Deception Pass/San Juans. 256: maximum energy (E=8), Pacific winter swell, outer coast. Shore energy at opposite ends of the scale.

s36_pair.pairing_note: E=8 storm surf vs E=6 stoner rock. The storm is bigger than the music. This is intentional: the storm arc opens at E=8 while the S36 side holds at E=6. The stoner rock riff is heavy and sustained; the Pacific winter storm is categorically larger. The music continues anyway -- in a rehearsal space in Seattle, 150 miles from Cape Disappointment, the riff goes on while the ocean reaches its maximum expression.

milestones: FIRST E=8 ENTRY IN SPS SERIES
